message:
The other day, as I was preparing for a possible storm, I found myself rummaging through the closet and stumbled upon a dusty old board game. You know the kind—no batteries required, just a good ol’ dose of competition and laughter! 

As the winds picked up outside, my family and I gathered around the table, and it hit me: sometimes the best way to weather a storm is to unplug and reconnect (literally and figuratively). We laughed, shared stories, and made memories I wouldn’t trade for anything. 

It was a delightful reminder that even in a world of tech, low-tech fun can be a lifesaver during unexpected times. Have you rediscovered any old-school activities that bring your family together? This little refresher helped!
